vision.title: Control9 Policy Packs
vision.mission: Give Control9 customers trustworthy baseline controls for common infrastructure risk so they can move from shadow-mode assessment to enforce-mode governance without writing every rule from scratch.
vision.core_purpose: This repo owns versioned policy packs and semantic classifiers for CDK/CloudFormation and Terraform/OpenTofu. It translates repeated Alto9 assessment findings into deterministic policies that produce allow, deny, approval-required, and observe decisions with clear reasons and evidence defaults.
vision.key_architecture: Policy packs should be versioned, testable, and readable. They cover friendly policy documents, compiled rule definitions, classifier fixtures, examples, and metadata for production mutation, IAM privilege expansion, new roles, cross-account access, destructive changes, secrets exposure hints, unapproved pipeline sources, cloud cost thresholds, and off-path CloudTrail findings.

strategic_goals.short_term: Define the built-in MVP policy pack and baseline classifiers for CDK/CloudFormation and Terraform/OpenTofu: IAM expansion, new role, destructive change, network boundary change, production target, secrets exposure hints, and unapproved pipeline source.
strategic_goals.medium_term: Add policy versioning, dry-run fixtures, tenant policy configuration examples, exception patterns, deploy verification rules, and assessment report mappings so SaaS decisions are deterministic and explainable.
strategic_goals.long_term: Support managed controls and enterprise libraries with curated packs, custom policy authoring, exception workflows, SIEM/GRC-friendly metadata, retention defaults, and optional OPA/Rego import only if enterprise buyers need it.

key_principles.text: Policies should express infrastructure governance meaning, not force users to reason only in low-level syntax.
Every decision must carry a policy version, matched rule, risk summary, clear reason, and enough fixture coverage to be repeatable in tests and production.

Baseline packs should make common production risk boring: IAM expansion requires approval, destructive production changes deny by default, and unmatched CloudTrail mutations are observed as high-severity off-path events.
Policy packs must work in shadow mode and enforce mode. The same input envelope should produce the same semantic result regardless of whether the product blocks the action.
Do not make OPA/Rego the default product contract. Import or interop can come later if it helps enterprise buyers.

competitive_advantages.text: Curated policy packs turn Alto9 implementation experience into repeatable SaaS controls customers can adopt quickly.
Semantic classifiers help customers see the infrastructure risks platform leads care about, instead of raw diff noise.

differentiation_from_competitors.text: Unlike generic policy-as-code libraries, Control9 packs are tied to action envelopes, approvals, artifact fingerprints, and evidence timelines.
Unlike audit-only tools, the same pack can explain shadow findings, require human approval, deny unsafe production work, and flag off-path cloud mutations.
